source_document.document_identity_notes: Regulator-source PDF from Wisconsin DFI. Cover page confirms Spring-Green Lawn Care Corp. 2025 FDD. Issuance date: March 31, 2025. Franchise also offers pest control services.

initial_franchise_fee.notes: $45,000 for territory up to 60,000 SFDUs. Industry Associate Program: reduced to $27,000. Additional SFDUs over 60,000: $0.50 each. $30,000 due at signing, $15,000 due before training ($15,000 may be financed over 5 years). VetFran: up to $5,000 credit toward start-up expenses. Additionally, initial marketing campaign fee of $35,000 and initial property data fee up to $16,500 ($0.55/SFDU, minimum 50% of territory measured) are required separate initial payments.

provenance.exact_text: you must pay an initial franchise fee of $45,000 for a geographic territory containing up to 60,000 single family dwelling units

royalty_rate.notes: Tiered by annual cumulative Gross Sales: First Tier (up to $250K): 10%; Second Tier ($250K-$500K): 9%; Third Tier (over $500K): 8%. Industry Associate Program first lawn care season: 5%/4%/3%. Multi-territory franchisees may aggregate Gross Sales across territories. Renewals may have different rates. Payable weekly, 28 days from end of reporting week.

provenance.exact_text: Sliding scale of 10% to 8% of Gross Sales of the Franchised Business

advertising_and_marketing.structure_description: National Advertising Fund: 2% of Gross Sales. Regional Advertising: up to 2% additional (not currently collected). Local Marketing minimum required but shortfall paid to franchisor if not met.

components.name: Regional Advertising Fee
components.notes: Up to 2% of Gross Sales. Not currently collected.

components.name: Local Marketing Shortfall
components.notes: If local marketing expenditures are less than minimum required amount, franchisee must pay shortfall to franchisor for contribution to the Fund.

advertising_and_marketing.notes: Effective rate is 2% nationally. Regional fee (up to 2% additional) is authorized but not currently imposed.

provenance.item_reference: Item 6, Advertising Fund and Regional Advertising rows
provenance.exact_text: 2% of Gross Sales of the Franchised Business

technology_fee.notes: $235/month plus individual license costs: Office Online License $16/user/month or Full Office License $29/user/month (minimum 1 Full Office License required). Subject to Fee Adjustment (up to 100% increase annually).

provenance.item_reference: Item 6, Technology Fee row and Note 8
provenance.exact_text: $235 per month plus individual license cost.

transfer_fee.formula: 2.5% of Gross Sales of the Franchised Business in the 12 months preceding the transfer, minimum $7,500, maximum $17,500.
transfer_fee.notes: Payable before or at closing of approved transfer.

provenance.exact_text: 2½% of Gross Sales of the Franchised Business within the 12 months immediately preceding the effective date, but no less than $7,500 and no more than $17,500

other_fees.notes: $200-$1,950/month. Mandatory for first 2 years, case-by-case after that. Cost depends on number of customers.

provenance.item_reference: Item 6, Call Center Services row and Note 14

other_fees.notes: $497-$692/month depending on equipment chosen. Not required to lease from franchisor. Purchase option: ~$27,596 for lawn care equipment.
$650 plus expenses for national meeting. Attendance mandatory. Lodging/meals may be covered for regional meetings. Subject to Fee Adjustment.

provenance.item_reference: Item 6, National or Regional Meeting Fee row

other_fees.notes: For Online Learning Center access. May include NALP membership. Subject to Fee Adjustment.
$20/month for 8 months. Subject to Fee Adjustment.

provenance.item_reference: Item 6, National Advisory Council Assessment Fee row

other_fees.notes: Currently $0.55 per SFDU for additional/updated property data collection.
Franchisor may debit 120% of 4-week average if sales not reported.

line_items.notes: $27,000 for Industry Associate Program. $15,000 may be financed over 5 years.
Includes 20% down payment on leased equipment. Vehicle ~$53,998, typically $0 down financed over 5 years. Lawn care equipment purchase option: ~$27,596.
Used entirely for marketing/advertising in territory. Includes prospect database, direct response, media advertising.
$0.55/SFDU, minimum 50% of territory measured. May be financed over 5 years.
$0 if virtual training. $1,365-$1,835 for in-person travel/living costs.
Includes licenses, insurance, professional services, prepaid expenses. Lower end assumes Industry Associate with existing licenses.
Payroll (excluding owner), supplies, insurance, vehicle costs, software fees, royalties, advertising, lease payments. New businesses usually generate negative cash flow.

tables.description: Gross Profit Margin Results as a Percentage of Gross Sales for Franchised Businesses in operation 1+ full year
tables.population: 62 of 71 Franchised Businesses operating 1+ full year as of Dec 31, 2024, under same ownership. These 62 operated 113 of 126 franchised territories. 5 Franchised Businesses excluded due to insufficient data; 4 not yet 1 full year.

material_costs.note: Lower is better for expense categories

tables.notes: Gross Profit Margin = 100% - Cost of Sales. Includes direct labor costs (unlike Lawn Doctor's Table C which only deducts materials). This makes Spring-Green's 68.1% not directly comparable to Lawn Doctor's 85.4% — Spring-Green includes labor in COGS. Accounting method varies (cash vs accrual). Some franchisees may misallocate admin employees to direct labor.

tables.description: Annual Gross Sales Results for Franchised Businesses in operation 1+ full year
tables.population: 71 Franchised Businesses operating 1+ full year as of Dec 31, 2024. These 71 operated 386 full-time production vehicles.
tables.notes: Gross Sales reported per Franchised Business (may include multiple territories). Single-territory subset (60,000 or fewer SFDUs): 5 businesses, average $587,457, median $569,577. Revenue per customer tightly distributed ($393-$957). Note 3 missing from extracted tables — count of 32 multi-territory + 36 single = 68, not 71; 3 businesses likely fell between categories.

tables.description: Marketing Investment, New Services Revenue, and Customer Retention during 2024
tables.population: 66 of 71 Franchised Businesses operating 1+ full year and participating in approved marketing campaign. Also broken out for 36 single-territory businesses.
tables.notes: Customer retention of 81.3% is comparable to Lawn Doctor. Marketing ROI of $2.62 per $1 invested is a useful efficiency metric. 5 businesses excluded due to insufficient data.

item_19_financial_performance.notes: Item 19 disclosed with 3 tables covering cost structure, revenue, and marketing effectiveness. Gross Profit Margin includes direct labor (unlike Lawn Doctor which only deducts materials) — not directly comparable across brands without adjustment. Revenue per customer ($556 avg) and customer retention (81.3%) are useful cross-brand metrics. All data based on franchisee-submitted financial information also used for royalty calculation.

years_reported.notes: Item 20 counts territories, not physical locations. 126 franchised territories operated by fewer than 126 businesses. 26 affiliate-owned territories (Superior Lawns) operated from 6 locations. Franchised: 126→128 (+2).

Franchised: 128→124 (-4). 2 terminations (LA, MI), 2 non-renewals (LA), 2 reacquired (WI). Affiliate Superior Lawns reacquired 2 territories from franchisees (WI). Louisiana exited entirely: 3 territories → 0.

Franchised: 124→126 (+2). Affiliate Superior Lawns reacquired 2 more territories (WI). 72 Franchised Businesses operated 126 territories. Projected 4 new franchised territories in 2025 (KY, OH, OK, PA). Some franchisees signed confidentiality clauses restricting them from speaking openly about their experience.

item_3_litigation.notes: No litigation required to be disclosed. No bankruptcy either (Item 4).

provenance.exact_text: No litigation is required to be disclosed in this Item.
We are a Delaware corporation organized in January 1977... We have offered franchises similar to the franchise described in this Disclosure Document since 1977.

warnings: Royalty is tiered (10%/9%/8%) — recorded with field_status 'ambiguous'.
Item 20 counts territories, not physical locations or businesses. 72 Franchised Businesses operated 126 territories as of Dec 31, 2024.

Affiliate Superior Lawns (30 territories, 6 locations) is counted separately in Table 4 but included in total outlet count. This is not company-owned in the traditional sense — it's an affiliate operating under a franchise agreement.
Item 19 Table A Gross Profit Margin (68.1%) includes direct labor costs unlike Lawn Doctor's similar table which only deducts materials (85.4%). These are NOT comparable without adjustment.
Some franchisees signed confidentiality clauses restricting them from speaking openly about their experience — a notable disclosure.
Fee Adjustment clause allows up to 100% annual increase on several fees (technology, education, meeting, reporting).
Initial Property Data Fee ($16,500) is unusual — charged for SGE to measure and collect property data in territory. Not seen in other brands.
Transfer fee is revenue-based (2.5% of prior 12 months, floor $7,500, cap $17,500) — recorded as extracted with formula.

null_fields.reason: Formula-based: 2.5% of trailing 12-month Gross Sales, min $7,500, max $17,500.
